titleStmt.title: SII 41.92: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 92. (A.R. No. 91 of 1905.) Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District. On the north wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.

summary.p: Related to the following inscription. Records that ūrār of Tiruvāppanūr sold a village called Dēvaṉ alias Dēsappugaḻ-nallūr, freed from all taxes, to the temple of Tiruvanantīśvaram-uḍaiyanāyanār.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śvarappiḷḷaiyāṟkum Innāṭṭu tiruvāppanūrā yicainta vūrom cakkarapāṇi pūvaṉāna tenṉavan yiṭaikuḷa nāṭṭaraiyaṉum nāṇmālai kūttanāṉa Aḻakiyappāṇṭiya Arumpūr nāṭāḷvāṉum kaḷvaṉnāṇmālaiyāṉa kulacekaraccetiyarāyaṉum velavantevaṉāṉa teṉṉavaṉ peraiyaṉum Uyyavantāṉ muttanāṉa toṉṟiyarāyam ma ṭai Uyyavantānāṉa māyan muttaraiyaṉum nārāyaṇaṉtevanāṉa karukuṭiyarāyaṉum māṭa raṉparacu pāṇṭi muttaraiyaṉ
lāvatu Iṉṉāṭṭu tevaṉāṉa tecappukaḷnallūṟ vaḷaiyiṟ cuṟṟum viṟṟukkoṭuttataṟ kellai kūvvaṉ kiḻellai karaiyakkuḷattellaikku meṟkun tennellai minaṅkuṭina nirkovaikku vaṭakkum melellai nakkaneriyellaikkum nakkaṉeriyiṟk kiḻakkaṇṭaraikkum pontu vaṭakkum Arumuṉai virakkoṉ vayakkiyuḻuta ceykku teṉvarampu peṟṟa Āyattukku kkaleppamaṅka Āyattukkum kāṭaṉṉeri Ellaikkum kiḻakku vaṭavellai kāṭaṉeri yellaikup poṉa
ṭu kāṭum paṭituṟaiyumm maṟṟumeppeyaṟpaṭṭatu miṟaiyili muṟcūṭṭāka candrādityavaṟ celvatākavum Emmilicainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikku viṟṟupporuḷaṟak koṇṭu viṟṟu vilaikalañ ceytu koṭuttom nāyaṉār tiruvaṉandīśvaramuṭaināyaṉār koyilil Ādicaṇdheśvarappiḷḷaiyār tiruvāppāṉūr Ūrāyicainta Ūrom Ivvempalāl vanta kaṭamai kuṭimai kāṇikkai viṉiyokam Antarāyam Eccoṟu cilvari cetāram tiruveḻuttu Ukavaiōlai muṭṭāvāḷ pañcupili cantivikkiramappeṟu kalai
Emmilicainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikkuṟa viṟṟu poruḷaṟak koṇṭu viṟṟu vilaipramāṇam paṇṇikkoṭutōm tiruvāppaṉūr Ūrāyicanta Ūrom tiruvanandīmuṭaiya nayiṉār koyilil Ādicaṇḍheśvarappiḷḷaiyāṟku Ippaṭikkivai
Digital edition of SII 41.92 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
